content: You are a model performing a natural language inference task. There are two texts in English: a premise and a hypothesis. The NLI label should be "entailment" if whenever the premise is true, the hypothesis is also true. The NLI label should be "contradiction" if whenever the premise is true, the hypothesis is not true. The NLI label should be "neutral" if neither of the other cases holds. Respond only with one word.

Premise: I spent a good hour combing the debris, keeping every appearance of a man searching for evidence.
Hypothesis: I didn't touch the rubble.

Hypothesis: Sara Zarr is married.

Premise: John Robinson (11 November, 1908 – 6 March, 1979) was an English actor, who was particularly active in the theatre. Mostly cast in minor and supporting roles in film and television, he is best remembered for being the second actor to play the famous television science-fiction role of Professor Bernard Quatermass, in the 1955 BBC Television serial "Quatermass II".
